rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Peek at the next module before adding a second exercise
Between-module reading appears in two places
Cross-module artefact contracts must be explicit at both ends
Session boundaries and working directories are designed, not implicit
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Re-fire after body fix. M2 `## Next` now closes with: 'Close this session when the second read lands; Module 3 opens fresh in the repo holding the small feature you'll bring.' Names (a) close = end session at end of second read, (b) open = fresh session in the repo carrying the M3-prework feature. M3 `## Start here` link-walks to `curriculum/exercises/open-the-side-quest.md` which carries the canonical `**Session** *(new, "m3-security")*` widget and 'Start a new Claude Code session in your repo' — naming the working directory explicitly as the main lane (same repo carried from M1/M2 holding the M3 feature). Working-directory continuity is stated at both ends; the sibling worktree at `../<repo>-m3-quality` is a deliberate side-quest fork (not the M2→M3 boundary move), with rationale visible in the exercise body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artefact-contract maintainer-block rows at both ends
Cross-module structural-failure rescue callouts at module open

rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M2 prework carries the canonical form ('Come to Module 2 without a surfaced task and you'll be scrambling to find one while the room is already in plan mode. Your call.'). M3's `## Bring to Module 4` section also carries the canonical form for the M3→M4 boundary. M2→M3 prework framing differs structurally (Too small / Too large / Pick the size that fits) but doesn't violate the rule — the rule fires on bring-or-scramble lines that exist, not on a mandate that every prework include one.
